ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a paratha concept (FOODON:03302757) that is the closest parent match to Roti Canai. No exact Malaysian Roti Canai concept exists in FoodOn as of 2026. A new concept is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Unleavened bread — pan-fried layered flatbread (paratha style) (closest parent)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A01VX is the closest defensible parent for pan-fried layered flatbread. No exact Roti Canai code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2026.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for Roti Canai or Malaysian flatbread identified as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of plain roti canai weighs approximately 100 g and is the typical single serving at a Malaysian mamak stall.

nutrient_source.title: Roti Canai — Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central paratha data (SR Legacy #18364) for secondary micronutrients
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD); cross-reference: USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary macronutrient and core micronutrient values sourced from IMR Malaysia MyFCD Roti Canai entry. Secondary micronutrients (selenium, phosphorus, zinc, B-vitamins) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy paratha (pan-fried, #18364) for plausibility verification. Values are consistent across both sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of White Flour Paratha (pan-fried) — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous to Singapore plain prata
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 62 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white flour paratha (pan-fried), which is the closest analogous food to Roti Canai. The same GI category applies to Singapore plain prata. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (62 × 40.2) / 100 ≈ 24.9 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 380 mg per 100 g piece (~17% of the 2300 mg daily recommended limit); two pieces commonly consumed at a sitting would contribute ~34% of the daily sodium limit from roti alone.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to one piece per meal and avoid high-sodium accompaniments such as salted fish curry to keep total sodium intake within the 2300 mg daily target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flour base with a medium GI of 62 and a high glycemic load of approximately 25 per piece; the high fat content from ghee slows but does not prevent a significant postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit plain roti canai to one piece per meal, pair it with a high-fibre accompaniment such as dhal, and consider substituting with a whole-wheat paratha to reduce the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 5.2 g and trans fat 0.2 g per 100 g; regular consumption of ghee-enriched refined-flour flatbread contributes to LDL cholesterol elevation and cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit roti canai to occasional consumption, request preparation with minimal ghee or a plant-based fat substitute, and balance the meal with vegetables and lean protein to reduce overall saturated fat intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined wheat flour is pro-inflammatory at high consumption frequencies; moderate saturated fat from ghee may exacerbate systemic inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should limit roti canai to occasional consumption and prefer whole-grain alternatives to reduce dietary pro-inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roti Canai (also called roti prata in Singapore) is a Malaysian Indian Muslim flatbread made from refined wheat flour dough enriched with ghee or margarine. The dough is repeatedly folded, stretched, and tossed to develop multiple thin, flaky layers, then cooked on a well-greased flat griddle until golden-brown and crispy on the outside with a soft, chewy interior. The plain (kosong) version contains no filling and is traditionally served with dhal curry, fish curry, or chicken curry for dipping. It is the most popular breakfast item at Malaysian mamak stalls and is widely enjoyed in Singapore, where it is called plain prata. Nutritionally, one standard piece (100 g) provides approximately 302 kcal, 6.8 g protein, 13.5 g fat (of which 5.2 g is saturated), and 40.2 g carbohydrate. The medium glycemic index of 62 and high glycemic load of ~25 per piece make it a food of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The sodium content of 380 mg per piece requires attention for those with hypertension. Roti canai is vegetarian and halal, and is a culturally significant dish across Malaysia and Singapore.
common_user_queries: how many calories in roti canai
is roti canai the same as roti prata
can a diabetic eat roti canai
roti canai vs chapati which is healthier
how much sodium in roti canai

confidence_reason: Primary macronutrient values sourced directly from the IMR Malaysia MyFCD database, which is an approved primary regional source for Malaysian dishes. Secondary micronutrients cross-verified against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy paratha data. GI value sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest analogous food (white flour pan-fried paratha). All values are internally consistent and plausible for the described preparation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Roti Canai is not reported in the IMR Malaysia MyFCD database or USDA FoodData Central paratha entry as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for Roti Canai is not reported in the IMR Malaysia MyFCD database as of 2026. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — refined wheat flatbread contains no ascorbic acid; confirmed by IMR MyFCD and USDA SR Legacy paratha data). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no vitamin D-fortification in traditional roti canai preparation; confirmed by IMR MyFCD). All core macros (protein_g 6.8, fat_g 13.5, carbohydrate_g 40.2) are non-zero and fully supported. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate based on white flour pan-fried paratha from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct Roti Canai GI measurement was identified in the database as of 2026. This food is also widely known as plain prata in Singapore.
